title: Airbnb's Earnings: Sky High Results, Grounded Guidance?
text: Airbnb's stock surged over 14% on February 14, 2025, following strong Q4 2024 earnings that exceeded analyst expectations across multiple metrics. Q4 2024 Performance: Revenue reached $2.50 billion, surpassing the $2.42 billion forecast. Earnings per share hit $0.73 versus the expected $0.61. Net income totaled $461 million with a 19% margin. Free cash flow generated $458 million. Gross Booking Value (GBV), a critical metric reflecting the total dollar value of bookings, reached $17.6 billion, a 13% year-over-year increase. Strategic Drivers: The company attributed success to product improvements like Guest Favorites and the Co-Host Network, alongside global expansion efforts in Latin America and Asia Pacific. Mobile bookings represented 60% of total transactions. Forward Outlook: However, management projected Q1 2025 revenue between $2.23–$2.27 billion, representing only 4–6% growth. The company attributed this to unfavorable calendar comparisons and foreign exchange headwinds, though underlying growth remained stronger at 10–12%.
